proj.thebeebaby.h1: TheBeeBaby: Digital marketplace for baby gear rental
proj.thebeebaby.subtitle: A web platform connecting families traveling with babies to local providers—book strollers, car seats, cribs, high chairs and more by city, dates, and real availability.
proj.thebeebaby.scope: Scope: Product design, UX, bookings, payments & multi-sided operations
proj.thebeebaby.valueDesc: TheBeeBaby lets traveling families rent safe baby gear at their destination instead of hauling bulky items on every trip—while local providers monetize inventory through a single marketplace with real calendars, secure payments, and clear operational workflows.
proj.thebeebaby.problemDesc: Families with babies need to travel without carrying heavy gear—while still accessing safe, available products at their destination. The platform had to unify inventory, cities, providers, calendars, and payments in one reliable product experience.
proj.thebeebaby.capabilitiesLabel: Beyond a catalog, the marketplace includes:
proj.thebeebaby.cap1: Separate experiences for travelers, providers, and the operations team
proj.thebeebaby.cap2: Visual catalog with availability, pricing, cities, and delivery areas
proj.thebeebaby.cap3: Bookings, secure payments, and operational reporting
proj.thebeebaby.processIntro: I owned the product end to end—from architecture to production-ready marketplace infrastructure. Here is how it was built:
proj.thebeebaby.step1Desc: Public discovery, private dashboards, and modular services for catalog, bookings, and payments—organized for scale, maintainability, and a clear separation between public and operational areas.
proj.thebeebaby.step2Desc: Structured inventory by city and calendar availability, with consistent location data so families and providers always see accurate listings and coverage.
proj.thebeebaby.step3Desc: Responsive interfaces for search, booking, account access, and provider management—with fast catalog browsing and conversion-focused flows on mobile and desktop.
proj.thebeebaby.step4Desc: End-to-end reservation flow integrated with a trusted payment provider, with business rules enforced on the server and inventory updated only after confirmed bookings.
proj.thebeebaby.step5Desc: Secure sign-in and permission levels so each user type only accesses what they need—protecting accounts, provider data, and day-to-day operations.
proj.thebeebaby.techIntro: Built for real traffic and payments—reliable, fast, and trustworthy for families and providers.
proj.thebeebaby.feat1Desc: Optimized delivery for high traffic: efficient asset loading, paginated data handling, and smart caching so the platform stays responsive during peak travel seasons.
proj.thebeebaby.feat2Desc: Consistent product and location data across the marketplace, with server-enforced business rules before any booking or payment is confirmed.
proj.thebeebaby.feat3Desc: Access controls for private areas and operational tools, following security best practices without exposing how internal systems are wired.
proj.thebeebaby.mockupsDesc: Hero, catalog, provider dashboard, and secure login—designed for trust, speed, and conversion on every device.
proj.thebeebaby.resultsDesc: TheBeeBaby turns a real family mobility problem into a polished digital experience: geolocated catalog, bookings, payments, operational dashboards, international SEO, and day-to-day reliability. Families find gear at destination; providers manage inventory and orders; the brand gains a scalable channel for growth.
proj.dahuss.h1: Dahuss Homes: Full-stack real estate platform
proj.dahuss.subtitle: A platform to publish, search, and manage premium properties across Spain, Mexico, and Latin America—with a public experience built to convert and a secure admin panel to run the business.
proj.dahuss.scope: Scope: Product design, public site, admin tools, lead capture & cloud deploy
proj.dahuss.valueDesc: Dahuss centralizes the property catalog, presents listings professionally, captures qualified inquiries, and manages inventory from one secure private panel—replacing scattered spreadsheets and disconnected tools.
proj.dahuss.problemDesc: A high-end real estate business needs to showcase valuable properties clearly, visually, and with powerful filters. Before this build, listings, images, inquiries, and sales statuses lived in manual or disconnected workflows. Dahuss unifies everything in one web product.
proj.dahuss.cap1: Publish properties for sale or rent
proj.dahuss.cap2: Filter by country, zone, type, price, bedrooms, and market
proj.dahuss.cap3: Property detail pages with images, features, and inquiry forms
proj.dahuss.cap4: Lead capture tied to specific listings
proj.dahuss.cap5: Private admin for properties and commercial inquiries
proj.dahuss.processIntro: I owned the product end to end—from structure to a deployment-ready platform. Here is how it was built:
proj.dahuss.step1Desc: Public discovery, private admin areas, and modular services for listings, inquiries, and media—organized for scale and a clear split between public and operational workflows.
proj.dahuss.step2Desc: Structured property and lead information with pricing, location, publish state, galleries, and amenities—designed for fast filtering across countries, zones, and commercial criteria.
proj.dahuss.step3Desc: Home, catalog, search, property detail pages, and inquiry forms—with featured listings, text search, commercial filters, and bilingual ES/EN for international audiences.
proj.dahuss.step4Desc: Inquiry flows with server-enforced validation, consistent responses, and paginated browsing—ensuring leads are tied to real, published listings.
proj.dahuss.step5Desc: Create, edit, publish, feature, or remove properties; review leads and update status—from a protected workspace for the agency team.
proj.dahuss.step6Desc: Optimized uploads with client-side resizing and compression before storage—plus sensible limits on count and file size to keep operations fast and reliable.
proj.dahuss.mockupsDesc: Public catalog, property experience, secure login, and admin dashboards—designed for premium listings and daily operations.
proj.dahuss.techIntro: Built for production traffic—reliable performance, trustworthy data, and protected operational areas.
proj.dahuss.feat1Desc: Image optimization handled before upload; efficient browsing with pagination and caching strategies so the platform stays responsive under real traffic.
proj.dahuss.feat2Desc: Validated property information across public pages and admin tools—normalized amenities, services, and media so teams and visitors always see aligned content.
proj.dahuss.feat3Desc: Secure access for admin workflows and permission-aware operations—following security best practices without exposing how internal systems are wired.
proj.dahuss.resultsDesc: Dahuss Homes is a complete, modern real estate platform ready for production—an optimized public experience plus a functional, secure admin panel. The agency can publish listings, receive inquiries, manage leads, and keep inventory current without external tools. Stronger digital presentation, less operational friction, and a scalable base for new commercial features.
proj.dahuss.res1: Unified catalog, leads, and admin in one product
proj.dahuss.res2: International filters and bilingual public experience
proj.dahuss.res3: Secure operations, media workflow & dependable day-to-day use
proj.retorica.h1: Retórica Company: Web platform for events, marketing & experiences
proj.retorica.subtitle: A corporate site for an entertainment and experiential marketing ecosystem—five business units, immersive visuals, bilingual SEO, and a contact flow that turns visits into real inquiries.
proj.retorica.scope: Scope: Architecture, frontend, content system, contact workflow & deploy
proj.retorica.valueDesc: Retórica presents a multi-line events and marketing brand through one immersive experience—FEST, SOCIAL, PROM, NODO, and VENUES—communicating services, case imagery, positioning, and lead capture without scattering content across channels.
proj.retorica.problemDesc: Retórica needed a professional digital presence for festivals, graduations, influencer marketing, brand activations, and venue management—turning scattered brand content into a fast, commercially effective web experience with high-impact imagery, clear business-unit differentiation, and a contact form that generates real requests.
proj.retorica.capabilitiesLabel: Technically, the build had to deliver:
proj.retorica.cap1: Maintainable content architecture with optimized media
proj.retorica.cap2: Static routes, SEO metadata, and bilingual support
proj.retorica.cap3: Immersive galleries, modals, and smooth navigation
proj.retorica.cap4: Light server load in production with a reliable contact flow
proj.retorica.processIntro: I delivered architecture through deployment—from static generation to the contact pipeline. Here is how it was built:
proj.retorica.step1Desc: A modern static-site framework for high performance—HTML, CSS, and JS delivered from CDN with minimal work on the server at request time.
proj.retorica.step2Desc: Astro components and Tailwind: hero, fixed navigation, sections per business unit, interactive galleries, descriptive modals, and contact block—with responsive layout, mobile menu, language switcher, smooth scroll, and scroll-triggered animations.
proj.retorica.step3Desc: A typed content system for blog and editorial posts—normalized titles, dates, categories, tags, and SEO fields so published pages and URLs stay consistent.
proj.retorica.step4Desc: A secure contact workflow that validates user input, protects message content, and delivers inquiries to the team through a trusted email service.
proj.retorica.step5Desc: Sitemap, Open Graph, global SEO config, bilingual routes, and build-time asset compression—deployed to modern cloud hosting with a lean production footprint.
proj.retorica.mockupsDesc: Hero, business units, immersive sections, and contact forms—designed for brand storytelling and lead conversion.
proj.retorica.techIntro: High visual impact with static-first performance—validated content, minimal server load, and a focused contact experience.
proj.retorica.feat1Desc: Heavy work happens at build: static pages, editorial content, route generation, and asset optimization. At runtime the site mainly serves static files and handles contact requests—with responsive images, lazy loading, and smooth in-browser animations.
proj.retorica.feat2Desc: A single validated content layer for site config, articles, images, CMS settings, and SEO—so editorial updates do not break URLs or the live experience.
proj.retorica.feat3Desc: Contact submissions are validated and cleaned before delivery; production hosting follows security best practices without publishing internal configuration details.
proj.retorica.resultsDesc: Retórica is a high-impact, technically optimized platform ready to represent a multi-line events brand—combining storytelling, static performance, validated content, and commercial capture in one experience. Fast, maintainable, and scalable: publish content, showcase services, receive inquiries, and deploy on modern infrastructure with low server consumption. For the brand, it works as a presentation card, business-unit catalog, and direct conversion channel for new projects.
proj.retorica.res1: Five business units unified in one immersive brand site
proj.retorica.res2: Fast static build with bilingual SEO & compression
proj.retorica.res3: Validated content pipeline and reliable inquiry delivery
proj.floreria.h1: Florería El Nuevo Pensamiento: Commercial website for a Valparaíso florist
proj.floreria.subtitle: A fast static site to showcase bouquets, wreaths, cushions, and in-store services—with WhatsApp contact, map location, and a visual catalog built for mobile discovery.
proj.floreria.scope: Scope: Design, frontend, interactions, local SEO & cloud deploy
proj.floreria.valueDesc: The site positions Florería El Nuevo Pensamiento online in Valparaíso—presenting floral products and in-person service clearly, driving direct WhatsApp inquiries, map visits, and visual exploration without a heavy app or complex backend.
proj.floreria.problemDesc: The florist needed a clear, fast, trustworthy digital presence for products, hours, location, and contact channels—without relying on a heavy application or custom server. The goal was to turn a traditionally in-person and social-media-driven offer into an organized, indexable, mobile-friendly web experience.
proj.floreria.cap1: Visual catalog with collapsible sections and image modals
proj.floreria.cap2: Direct WhatsApp, phone, and Google Maps integration
proj.floreria.cap3: Institutional video, FAQ, and Instagram feed
proj.floreria.cap4: Local SEO with structured data for search engines
proj.floreria.processIntro: I delivered the full site from structure to production deployment. Here is how it was built:
proj.floreria.step1Desc: A lean static stack with organized assets by product category—no custom server, minimal complexity, and delivery through a global CDN.
proj.floreria.step2Desc: Clear page blocks: hero, local value proposition, video, services, catalog, contact, FAQ, Instagram, and footer—with semantic HTML, local SEO copy, and direct links to WhatsApp, phone, Maps, and social channels.
proj.floreria.step3Desc: CSS variables, mobile-first layout, product grids, service cards, horizontal carousels, image modal, and floating WhatsApp button—prioritizing fast reading and a floral brand aesthetic.
proj.floreria.step4Desc: Smooth scrolling, active section navigation, image modals, collapsible blocks, and carousels—lightweight logic without frameworks for the core experience.
proj.floreria.step5Desc: Meta descriptions, canonical URLs, social previews, geolocation hints, and structured data for local business and FAQs—plus sitemap and discovery files for search engines.
proj.floreria.step6Desc: Static hosting with clean URLs, image caching, and production security settings that limit risky scripts and embeds—without publishing how the site is configured.
proj.floreria.mockupsDesc: Homepage, catalog, services, and contact flows—designed for mobile discovery and quick WhatsApp conversion.
proj.floreria.techIntro: Maximum performance and discoverability for a local business—minimal infrastructure, consistent data, and safe third-party embeds.
proj.floreria.feat1Desc: Fully static delivery via CDN—no server rendering or dynamic queries. Lightweight client optimizations, lazy images, and carousels that activate only when their section is visible.
proj.floreria.feat2Desc: Aligned visible content with structured data: business name, address, phone, hours, products, and location—so visitors and search engines see the same information.
proj.floreria.feat3Desc: No private APIs or user accounts—the site uses production security settings and official embeds for maps, video, and social content, without handling sensitive credentials in the project itself.
proj.floreria.resultsDesc: A fast, clear, production-ready website that turns the florist's digital presence into a consultable, visual, indexable experience. Better local search discovery, centralized business information, frictionless WhatsApp contact, and a responsive interface built for customers looking for fresh flowers, custom arrangements, or in-store pickup in Valparaíso.
proj.floreria.res1: Static architecture with low resource use and fast loads
proj.floreria.res2: Structured local SEO and consistent NAP data
about.bio: I'm Andrés Irigoyen, Full-Stack Developer. I specialize in transforming traditional businesses into profitable digital platforms. I combine technical rigor with a direct commercial vision: I translate your business needs into efficient, scalable software that improves your profit margins and solves real operational problems.
testimonials.title: What my clients say
testimonials.1.text: We came with just an idea and Andrés built the entire marketplace from scratch. He delivered a complete platform with payment gateways, databases, and robust admin panels. Exceptional technical expertise.
testimonials.1.author: María López, Operations - Thebeebaby
testimonials.2.text: "We knew our business in Asia, but not how to digitize it. Andrés built the entire platform from scratch, automated our quotes, and left us with a perfectly running system without any headaches."
testimonials.2.author: Carlos Ramírez, Director - Dragonmart

proj.dragonmart.name: Dragonmart
proj.dragonmart.desc: Corporate platform for international sourcing from Asia.
proj.dragonmart.h1: Dragonmart: Corporate platform for international sourcing from Asia.
proj.dragonmart.valueProp: Dragonmart connects importing companies with suppliers, materials, and logistics processes in Asia, reducing technical, commercial, and operational uncertainty through supplier validation, documentary control, structured quoting, and direct communication.
proj.dragonmart.role: Web developer responsible for frontend architecture, responsive implementation, quote forms, serverless function for request submission, technical site validation, structured SEO, and preparation for Vercel deployment.
proj.dragonmart.stack: HTML5, CSS3, vanilla JavaScript, Node.js 20, Vercel, Serverless Functions, Resend API, JSON-LD, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, Web App Manifest, GitHub Actions.
proj.dragonmart.problem1: The project solves the need to present Dragon Mart Trading Inc as a professional sourcing, technical procurement, and import platform from Asia. The site had to work as a corporate showcase, but also as an operational tool: showing commercial divisions, explaining the import process, presenting visual evidence of projects, and allowing a client to request technical quotes with complete data.
proj.dragonmart.problem2: The solution is not limited to an informative landing page. It integrates specialized forms, clean routing, bilingual content, SEO structure, static asset validation, and a serverless API to convert commercial requests into operational emails sent to the Dragon Mart team.
proj.dragonmart.method1Desc: The project was built as a static site optimized for Vercel deployment. This decision reduced complexity, improved load times, and avoided unnecessary server processing. The structure separates main HTML pages, global styles, JavaScript logic, visual assets, validation scripts, and a serverless function under api/send-quote.js.
proj.dragonmart.method2Desc: I developed specific pages for home, contact, quotes, corporate profile, Dragon Glass, and Dragon Ceramic. The frontend organizes content into high-value sections: commercial proposal, import process, sourcing divisions, international network, compliance, deliverables, project gallery, and forms. The interface uses vanilla JavaScript for progressive animations with IntersectionObserver, mobile navigation, FAQ accordions, gallery modals, sliders, custom category selector, and automatic category preloading in quotes via URL parameters.
proj.dragonmart.method3Desc: The corporate and quote forms collect key data such as product, category, technical description, volume, port, name, company, and email. On the client, the information is packaged as JSON and sent to /api/send-quote. I also implemented a fallback with mailto:. If the email service is not configured or fails, the system automatically prepares the email in the user's client so the commercial opportunity is not lost.
proj.dragonmart.method4Desc: The api/send-quote.js function acts as a secure layer between the browser and Resend. It validates POST methods, reads the payload, normalizes fields, validates emails, checks mandatory fields, generates HTML and plain text versions of the email, and sends the request using the Resend API. Credentials are not exposed in the browser. The RESEND_API_KEY is read from environment variables and used only on the server via the Authorization: Bearer header.
proj.dragonmart.method5Desc: The i18n.js file allows switching between Spanish and English using a translation dictionary and persistence in localStorage. In addition, the site includes SEO metadata, Open Graph, Twitter Cards, JSON-LD, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, llms.txt, and site.webmanifest.
proj.dragonmart.feat1Desc: To avoid saturating the server's CPU, the architecture shifts almost all the work to the static model and Vercel's CDN. Pages do not require dynamic rendering on every visit. The serverless function only executes when the user submits a form. Furthermore, server processing is deliberately lightweight.
proj.dragonmart.feat2Desc: I implemented a validation script that verifies that mandatory files exist, that local links point to real resources, that each page has a title, description, canonical, robots, and that the JSON-LD blocks are valid. If anything breaks, the build fails before deployment.
proj.dragonmart.feat3Desc: Security was designed avoiding the exposure of sensitive credentials on the frontend. Real authentication to the external provider occurs on the server. The data sent by the user is escaped with escapeHtml before building the HTML email, reducing injection risks.
proj.dragonmart.resultsDesc: Dragonmart consolidated itself as a lightweight, fast, and technically controlled corporate platform. The result combines commercial presentation, lead capture, technical quoting, structured SEO, automated validation, and serverless deployment.
proj.dragonmart.res2: Organizes requests from the first contact
proj.dragonmart.res3: Reduces operational errors by requiring clear information
cta.dragonmart.title: Need to digitize your B2B or corporate processes?
cta.dragonmart.desc: I build solid operational platforms like Dragonmart—from commercial presentation to technical validations and automated emails (serverless). Let's structure your operations today.
testimonials.3.text: "He started with a blank page and delivered a store ready to sell. What I appreciate most is how he made sure local customers could find us easily on Google and that the site was lightning fast. A total lifesaver."
testimonials.3.author: Elena Martínez, Founder - Florería ENP
